groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Question, intended answer, audience, important points, author perspective, language, tone, length, and necessary context.

groups.title: Evidence & Credibility
groups.description: Supporting material, examples, experience details, credentials, and limitations without encouraging fabricated authority.

groups.title: Promotion & Disclosure
groups.description: Commercial relationships, links, affiliation disclosure, and proportional reader actions.

groups.title: Style & Output
groups.description: Answer structure, opening, reading level, verification notes, and exceptional requirements.

variables.label: Quora question
variables.placeholder: What question should the answer respond to?
variables.hint: Paste the exact question or write it as it appears on Quora.

help.what: Provide the quora question for this Quora answer.
help.why: The answer must respond directly to the exact question being asked.
help.example: Good example: What is the best way for solo founders to stay consistent with content?
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported conclusions, invented evidence, or changing the question into an easier one.

variables.label: Answer goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main purpose
variables.hint: Choose what the answer should primarily help the reader do or understand.

help.what: Provide the answer goal for this Quora answer.
help.why: Different Quora questions require different reasoning and content structures.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported conclusions, invented evidence, or changing the question into an easier one.

variables.label: Target reader
variables.placeholder: For example: first-time founders, students, new homeowners
variables.hint: Describe who should find the answer most useful.

help.what: Optional context for the target reader field.
help.why: Reader knowledge and circumstances affect terminology, examples, and depth.
help.example: Good example: Solo founders building a small content workflow.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake testimonials, hidden promotion, engagement bait, or unsupported high-stakes advice.

variables.label: Main answer
variables.placeholder: State the main conclusion or position the answer should communicate.
variables.hint: Give the direct answer in your own words before adding supporting detail.

help.what: Provide the main answer for this Quora answer.
help.why: The model should not guess the user's intended conclusion or recommendation.
help.example: Good example: A simple repeatable system is more reliable than waiting for motivation.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported conclusions, invented evidence, or changing the question into an easier one.

variables.label: Key points to include
variables.placeholder: List the facts, reasons, warnings, steps, or distinctions that must appear.
variables.hint: Include only points that are important to answering the question.

help.what: Provide the key points to include for this Quora answer.
help.why: These points determine the substance and usefulness of the answer.
help.example: Good example: Use templates, define a weekly cadence, save prompts that work, and review which drafts become publishable.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported conclusions, invented evidence, or changing the question into an easier one.

variables.label: Author perspective
variables.placeholder: Choose the perspective
variables.hint: Select the truthful position from which the answer should be written.

help.what: Provide the author perspective for this Quora answer.
help.why: The model must know whether it may write from experience, expertise, opinion, or an organizational role.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported conclusions, invented evidence, or changing the question into an easier one.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: For example: English, Ukrainian, Spanish
variables.hint: Enter the language for the complete generated answer.

help.what: Provide the output language for this Quora answer.
help.why: Quora operates across languages, and the desired language cannot always be inferred.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported conclusions, invented evidence, or changing the question into an easier one.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Select a tone
variables.hint: Choose the overall voice without changing the factual position.

help.what: Optional context for the tone field.
help.why: Tone materially affects how credible and appropriate the answer feels to its intended reader.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake testimonials, hidden promotion, engagement bait, or unsupported high-stakes advice.

variables.label: Answer length
variables.placeholder: Select the preferred depth
variables.hint: The model may write less when the available facts do not support a longer answer.

help.what: Optional context for the answer length field.
help.why: Quora answers can range from brief explanations to detailed expert responses.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake testimonials, hidden promotion, engagement bait, or unsupported high-stakes advice.

variables.label: Question context
variables.placeholder: Add assumptions, background, or ambiguity the answer should address.
variables.hint: Use this when the question needs context beyond its visible wording.

help.what: Optional context for the question context field.
help.why: Some questions contain hidden assumptions or refer to a specific situation that affects the answer.
help.example: Good example: The reader is asking about consistency, not viral growth or paid ads.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake testimonials, hidden promotion, engagement bait, or unsupported high-stakes advice.

variables.label: Evidence and references
variables.placeholder: Paste approved facts, statistics, quotations, study details, or source links.
variables.hint: Only supplied evidence may be presented as sourced or verified.

help.what: Optional context for the evidence and references field.
help.why: Evidence-dependent answers require user-supplied facts and references to avoid fabrication.
help.example: Good example: Only use the supplied workflow notes; do not invent studies or statistics.
help.avoid: Avoid invented studies, statistics, quotations, sources, links, or claims that the supplied evidence does not support.

variables.label: Examples or practical steps
variables.placeholder: Add examples, procedures, comparisons, or steps that should be used.
variables.hint: Provide real details or clearly describe anything that should remain hypothetical.

help.what: Optional context for the examples or practical steps field.
help.why: Examples and procedures can materially improve explanatory, recommendation, and troubleshooting answers.
help.example: Good example: Pick one channel, create three reusable formats, and schedule one review block per week.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake testimonials, hidden promotion, engagement bait, or unsupported high-stakes advice.

variables.label: First-hand experience
variables.placeholder: Describe what you personally did, observed, used, or learned.
variables.hint: Include only genuine experience that may be written in the first person.

help.what: Optional context for the first-hand experience field.
help.why: The model needs specific authentic details before it can truthfully claim personal experience.
help.example: Good example: I used a saved prompt library to reduce rewriting before publishing weekly updates.
help.avoid: Avoid fake first-person experience, exaggerated outcomes, or stories that did not happen.

variables.label: Relevant credentials
variables.placeholder: For example: tax accountant in Ontario with eight years of experience
variables.hint: Add only credentials or roles directly relevant to the question.

help.what: Optional context for the relevant credentials field.
help.why: Relevant credentials can establish context but must not be inferred or invented.
help.example: Good example: Founder building content workflow tools.
help.avoid: Avoid credentials, roles, licenses, clients, employers, or authority signals that are not true and relevant.

variables.label: Limits and uncertainties
variables.placeholder: Note disputed facts, exceptions, risks, unknowns, or jurisdiction limits.
variables.hint: Use this to prevent an overly broad or overconfident conclusion.

help.what: Optional context for the limits and uncertainties field.
help.why: Known limitations may materially change the accuracy and safety of the answer.
help.example: Good example: The best cadence depends on the founder's available time and topic complexity.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake testimonials, hidden promotion, engagement bait, or unsupported high-stakes advice.

variables.label: Relationship to the subject
variables.placeholder: Select any relevant relationship
variables.hint: Disclose financial, professional, ownership, or other material connections.

help.what: Optional context for the relationship to the subject field.
help.why: Quora requires relevant affiliations to be disclosed clearly when they could affect how an answer is understood.
help.avoid: Avoid hiding material relationships or implying independent neutrality when a connection exists.

variables.label: Affiliated entity
variables.placeholder: Name the product, company, service, or organization.
variables.hint: This is used to create a clear and specific disclosure.

help.what: Optional context for the affiliated entity field.
help.why: A disclosure is not useful when it does not identify the relevant relationship or entity.
help.avoid: Avoid hiding material relationships or implying independent neutrality when a connection exists.

variables.label: Relevant link
variables.placeholder: https://www.creatornivo.com/resources
variables.hint: Include one link only when it directly helps answer the question.

help.what: Optional context for the relevant link field.
help.why: A relevant resource may improve the answer, but the model must not invent or overuse links.
help.avoid: Avoid invented, shortened, repeated, irrelevant, or promotional links that do not help answer the question.

variables.label: Reader next step
variables.placeholder: Choose an optional next step
variables.hint: The answer will use no CTA unless the selected action fits naturally.

help.what: Optional context for the reader next step field.
help.why: The desired next step affects the conclusion and determines whether a link or promotional mention is appropriate.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake testimonials, hidden promotion, engagement bait, or unsupported high-stakes advice.

variables.label: Answer structure
variables.placeholder: Select a structure
variables.hint: Choose how the explanation should be organized.

help.what: Optional context for the answer structure field.
help.why: Procedures, comparisons, and explanatory answers benefit from different structures.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake testimonials, hidden promotion, engagement bait, or unsupported high-stakes advice.

variables.label: Opening style
variables.placeholder: Select how the answer should begin
variables.hint: Quora readers should receive useful information immediately.

help.what: Optional context for the opening style field.
help.why: The opening determines whether the answer begins with a conclusion, clarification, or valid personal context.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake testimonials, hidden promotion, engagement bait, or unsupported high-stakes advice.

variables.label: Reading level
variables.placeholder: Select the expected knowledge level
variables.hint: This controls terminology and explanation depth rather than answer quality.

help.what: Optional context for the reading level field.
help.why: The same subject may require very different terminology for beginners and specialists.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake testimonials, hidden promotion, engagement bait, or unsupported high-stakes advice.

variables.label: Include verification notes
variables.placeholder: Off
variables.hint: Adds private source and claim-check notes after the public answer. Choose On or Off.

help.what: Choose whether to include private source and claim-check notes after the public answer.
help.why: Users working with factual or high-stakes material may need a separate publication review.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake testimonials, hidden promotion, engagement bait, or unsupported high-stakes advice.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Add necessary wording, exclusions, formatting, or other special instructions.
variables.hint: Use this only for requirements not already covered by another field.

help.what: Optional context for the additional requirements field.
help.why: One controlled miscellaneous field supports exceptional requirements without expanding the form unnecessarily.
help.example: Good example: Avoid fake results, user counts, revenue claims, and promotional pressure.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake testimonials, hidden promotion, engagement bait, or unsupported high-stakes advice.
